latex:
A fuel tank holds 200 gallons of gasoline when full. The tank is currently $\dispalystyle \frac{4}{5}$
full. If fuel costs \$5.50 per gallon, how many dollars worth of fuel are needed to fill the rest of the tank?
notes: }  \textbf{Answer:} If the tank is $\frac{4}{5}$ full, then it currently holds $\frac{4}{5} \times 200 = 160$ gallons of fuel, requiring 40 more gallons to fill the tank. This will cost $40 \times \$5.50 = \boxed{\$220}$. }
